_meta.source_paper: Ning, Y., Zhang, W., Yao, D., Xu, B., & Wijaya, T. T. (2025). Development and validation of the Artificial Intelligence Literacy Scale for Teachers (AILST). Education and Information Technologies, 30, 17769-17803.

removed_items.A1: I know how to use AI to integrate existing teaching resources.
removed_items.A2: I know how to use AI for teaching interaction.
removed_items.E2: I am able to perceive ethical and moral issues in the process of AI application keenly.
removed_items.E6: I am aware of the extent to which AI considers the individual differences of all students.

_meta.removal_reason: Removed by Ning et al. 2025 during EFA (factor loadings < 0.5 or cross-loadings on multiple factors). The retained 36-item set was validated via CFA (Cronbach alpha 0.851-0.943 across factors).
_meta.scoring_note: Storage is raw 1-5 with anchor 'Fully applicable' = 5. Reverse-scoring (scored = 6 - raw) applies ONLY to K1, A3, E3 (negation-framed items). After reversal, higher final score = higher TAIL (Teacher AI Literacy) per paper convention.

items.item_text: I believe that AI can enhance the efficiency of teaching preparation, such as lesson planning.
It is my assertion that AI has the capacity to improve both the efficiency and effectiveness of teaching.
I have knowledge of common AI technologies and platforms utilized in educational settings.
It is recognized that the integration of AI resources into the teaching process will impose new demands on teachers.
I acknowledge that AI is driving advancements in education and catalyzing pedagogical reforms.
I am proficient in grasping the functionalities of prevalent AI applications in education.
I am adept at utilizing commonly used AI tools in educational contexts.
I am proficient in utilizing domain-specific AI, such as mathematics intelligent tutoring systems.
I can choose the appropriate AI based on the teaching content of the subject I teach.
I believe I can help my colleagues to easily learn to use AI.
I am not fully versed in the common functionalities of AI in educational instruction, nor am I adept at discerning them.
I have knowledge of prevalent AI educational platforms and am proficient in their utilization.
I am committed to actively acquiring proficiency in new AI technologies and exploring their integration into my educational practices.
I understand how to integrate AI with instructional content and methodologies to enhance classroom efficiency and effectiveness.
I am capable of creating and simulating scenarios aligned with instructional content using AI.
Based on instructional content, I can select appropriate teaching methods and AI tools for instruction.
I can use personalized AI to select appropriate teaching methods to guide students in practice.
I am capable of leveraging AI to identify and address individual differences among students, as well as provide personalized guidance.
I engage in self-directed learning using AI to improve my understanding of disciplinary knowledge and educational theory.
I am able to visualize student performance with the assistance of AI and provide reasoned interpretations.
I don't know how to use AI for real-time feedback (i.e., providing immediate feedback on student performance after completing tasks).
I can anticipate potential problems in the process of AI application and develop solutions.
I can flexibly handle unexpected situations caused by AI failures in classroom teaching.
I can utilize AI to generate new teaching resources.
I can flexibly apply AI to create situations rich in creativity and inquiry.
I can innovate teaching models using AI.
I can master the application of AI in a certain subject and transfer it to other subjects.
I can use AI to update my own knowledge system in a subject.
I realize there may be ethical and moral issues with the application of AI.
I rely entirely on AI, and I believe AI can replace teachers.
Before teaching, I will carefully check AI to ensure the stability and reliability of the technology.
I hold a cautious attitude towards AI and will autonomously filter the resources provided by artificial intelligence.
I focus on protecting student privacy and using AI for autonomous monitoring with the consent of learners.
I pay attention to protecting the performance data analyzed by AI and will not disclose it to others.
I believe artificial intelligence has its pros and cons, and I will try to minimize the negative impact on teaching.
I will help students establish ethical and safety awareness of AI.
